prompt: How do I create a profile?
response: Creating your WorkForge profile is easy! Click on 'Profile' in the top navigation menu. Workers should add their skills, work experience, availability, and a professional photo. Employers should add company information, verification documents, and job posting preferences. A complete profile helps you get better matches!

prompt: How do I apply for jobs?
response: To apply for jobs: 1) Go to the Jobs page, 2) Use filters to find roles matching your skills and location, 3) Click on a job to see details, 4) Click 'Apply' and submit your proposal including your relevant experience and why you're a good fit. You can track all your applications in your dashboard.

prompt: What's the difference between worker and employer accounts?
response: Workers apply for jobs and offer their services, while employers post jobs and manage applicants. Workers build profiles showcasing skills and experience. Employers create company profiles and manage job listings. Both can message each other, but their dashboards and primary actions differ based on their role.

prompt: How do I start a conversation?
response: You can start conversations in several ways: 1) Go to Messages and click 'New Conversation', 2) From a worker or employer profile, click the 'Message' button, 3) From a job application, you can message the employer. All your conversations are organized in the Messages section for easy access.

prompt: Can I attach files to messages?
response: Yes! When composing a message, click the attachment icon (paperclip) to upload files. Supported formats include images (JPG, PNG), documents (PDF, DOC), and spreadsheets (XLS). There's a file size limit of 10MB per attachment. This is great for sharing work samples, contracts, or verification documents.

prompt: How do I improve my job matches?
response: To get better job matches: 1) Complete your profile with all skills and experience, 2) Keep your availability status updated, 3) Add relevant skills to your profile, 4) Set your preferred work location and radius, 5) Apply to jobs promptly, 6) Maintain a good rating by completing jobs successfully. The algorithm learns from your activity!

prompt: What is the AI assistant for?
response: The AI assistant helps you navigate WorkForge! It can answer platform questions, suggest next steps, help with messaging, guide you through verification, and provide onboarding support. Just type your question naturally - no need for special commands. The assistant learns from common questions to give better answers over time.

prompt: How do I report inappropriate messages?
response: To report inappropriate messages: 1) Open the conversation, 2) Click the three dots menu on the message, 3) Select 'Report', 4) Choose the reason (harassment, spam, scam, etc.), 5) Add details if needed. Our moderation team reviews reports within 24 hours. You can also block the user immediately from the same menu.

prompt: Can users with different roles message each other?
response: Yes! Workers, employers, and admins can all message each other based on platform permissions. Workers can message employers about jobs they've applied to. Employers can message workers who applied to their jobs. Admins can message anyone for support. This enables smooth communication throughout the hiring process.

prompt: How do I delete my own message?
response: To delete a message you sent: 1) Open the conversation, 2) Find your message, 3) Click the three dots menu, 4) Select 'Delete'. Note that deleted messages are removed from your view but may still be visible to the recipient and are retained in our system for safety and compliance purposes. Think before you send!

prompt: How do I post a job?
response: To post a job: 1) Click 'Post a Job' from your dashboard, 2) Fill in job title, description, and requirements, 3) Set location (on-site, remote, or hybrid), 4) Add salary or hourly rate range, 5) Select required skills, 6) Review and publish. Your job will be visible to matching workers immediately!

prompt: What payment methods are supported?
response: WorkForge supports multiple payment methods: 1) Platform Wallet (instant transfers between users), 2) Bank Transfer (ACH/direct deposit), 3) Mobile Money (M-Pesa, etc. where available), 4) Credit/Debit Cards for employer payments. All transactions are secured and tracked for your protection.

prompt: How does the rating system work?
response: After each completed job, both parties rate each other on a 1-5 star scale with optional written feedback. Ratings are averaged over time to show reliability. Consistent high ratings improve your visibility in search results. Low ratings or disputes are reviewed by our team. Ratings help build trust in the community!

prompt: Can I edit my profile after creating it?
response: Absolutely! You can edit your profile anytime by going to Profile > Edit. Update your skills, experience, availability, photos, and contact information. Keeping your profile current helps you get better job matches and shows employers you're active. Some changes may require re-verification.

prompt: How do I withdraw money from my wallet?
response: To withdraw: 1) Go to Wallet > Withdraw, 2) Select your preferred withdrawal method (bank transfer or mobile money), 3) Enter the amount, 4) Confirm your payment details, 5) Submit request. Bank transfers take 1-3 business days. Mobile money is usually within 24 hours. Minimum withdrawal is $10.

prompt: What happens if a job is cancelled?
response: If a job is cancelled: 1) Before work starts - no payment obligations, 2) After work starts - payment depends on cancellation reason and work completed, 3) Disputes are handled by our resolution team. Always communicate clearly about cancellations. Document any work completed for fair compensation.

prompt: How do I set my availability?
response: Set your availability in Profile > Availability. You can mark yourself as: Available (actively seeking work), Busy (not taking new jobs), or Away (temporarily unavailable). You can also set specific hours and days. Employers see your availability status when viewing your profile or considering you for jobs.

prompt: Is there a mobile app?
response: Yes! WorkForge has mobile apps for iOS and Android. Download from the App Store or Google Play. The app includes all core features: browsing jobs, applying, messaging, profile management, and payments. Enable notifications to stay updated on job matches and messages. Some advanced features are web-only.

prompt: How do I add skills to my profile?
response: Add skills in Profile > Skills. You can: 1) Search and select from our skill database, 2) Add custom skills if not listed, 3) Rate your proficiency level (Beginner, Intermediate, Expert), 4) Add certifications if you have them. Relevant skills help you appear in employer searches and job recommendations.

prompt: What are the fees for using WorkForge?
response: WorkForge charges a service fee on completed work: typically 10-15% depending on your plan and volume. Employers may pay a small posting fee for premium listings. There are no fees for browsing, applying, or messaging. All fees are clearly displayed before you accept any job or payment.

prompt: How do I contact support?
response: Contact support through: 1) Help Center (click ? icon), 2) Email: support@workforge.com, 3) In-app chat with AI assistant (24/7), 4) For urgent issues, use the 'Contact Admin' option in Messages. Response times: AI assistant (instant), email (within 24 hours), urgent (within 4 hours).

prompt: Can I work as both a worker and employer?
response: Yes! You can switch between worker and employer modes in your profile settings. This is useful if you sometimes hire help for your own projects while also offering services. Your ratings and history are maintained separately for each role. Just toggle the mode when you want to switch perspectives.

prompt: How do I verify my business as an employer?
response: Business verification requires: 1) Business registration documents, 2) Tax ID or equivalent, 3) Proof of address (utility bill or lease), 4) Authorized representative ID. Upload these in Settings > Verification. Verification typically takes 1-3 business days. Verified employers get a badge and priority support.

prompt: What should I include in my job proposal?
response: A strong proposal includes: 1) Personalized greeting mentioning the specific job, 2) Relevant experience and skills, 3) Why you're a good fit, 4) Your approach to the work, 5) Questions about the job (shows interest), 6) Professional closing. Keep it concise but informative. Tailor each proposal - generic ones get ignored!

prompt: How do I negotiate pay?
response: To negotiate pay: 1) Research market rates for similar work, 2) Consider your experience level, 3) Message the employer professionally with your rate and justification, 4) Be open to discussion and compromise, 5) Get agreement in writing before starting work. Fair negotiation is normal - just be respectful and realistic.

prompt: What is escrow and how does it work?
response: Escrow protects both parties: Employers deposit payment upfront, which is held by WorkForge until work is completed. Once the worker finishes and the employer approves, funds are released. If there's a dispute, our team reviews evidence before releasing funds. This ensures workers get paid and employers get quality work.

prompt: How do I dispute a payment?
response: To dispute a payment: 1) Go to the job details page, 2) Click 'Open Dispute', 3) Select the reason (non-payment, partial payment, quality issues), 4) Provide evidence (messages, photos, contracts), 5) Submit. Our dispute resolution team will review within 48 hours and may request additional information from both parties.

prompt: Can I see who viewed my profile?
response: Yes! Go to Profile > Insights to see profile views. You'll see: number of views, viewer types (employers/workers), and trends over time. Premium accounts see more detailed analytics including which companies viewed you. This helps you understand your visibility and adjust your profile for better results.

prompt: How do I pause my account?
response: To pause your account: Go to Settings > Account > Pause Account. You can set a return date or leave it indefinite. While paused: your profile is hidden from search, you won't receive job notifications, existing conversations remain accessible. You can reactivate anytime by logging in. No data is lost during pause.
